ai_analysis.one_sentence_summary: paymentservice still automounts the default service-account token even though payment microservices almost never call the Kubernetes API—disable automount unless you prove in-cluster clients.
ai_analysis.true_positive_rationale: Violations explicitly state the Deployment mounts tokens while using the default service account, matching the policy’s technical predicate.
ai_analysis.attack_vector: A compromised paymentservice pod could read the mounted token and attempt API calls permitted to that identity.
ai_analysis.blast_radius: Namespace-scoped to ms-demo unless default SA bindings are accidentally over-privileged cluster-wide.
ai_analysis.prioritization_rationale: MEDIUM policy but payment-tier adjacency nudges priority over generic microservices with the same configuration debt.
recommended_actions: Set automountServiceAccountToken: false on the paymentservice Pod template.
Introduce a dedicated ServiceAccount only if the app must talk to Kubernetes APIs.
Verify RBAC for namespace default SA is minimal before and after the change.
mitigation_strategies: Bundle the manifest tweak with upcoming payment-service releases to avoid emergency redeploy chatter.
